title: Emergent supersymmetry in a time-space inverted quantum mechanics
authors: Marcus W Beims, Arlans JS de Lara
abstract: This Letter shows that a supersymmetric structure is inherent to the time space inverted (TSI) quantum mechanics (QM) framework, where the spatial evolution of states is generated by the operator $\hat{\mathcal{P}}^{\pm}(\hat{\mathcal{H}},\hat t;q)=\pm\sqrt{2m[\hat{\mathcal{H}}-\mathcal{\hat V}(q)]}$ [\href{https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevA.95.032133}{Phys. Rev. A. {\bf 95}, 032133 (2017)}], named here Momentunian, whose square-root structure that can be factorized. Such factorization leads directly to a supersymmetric algebra with supercharges and partner Hamiltonians. For the relativistic Momentunian the zero mode states are shown to be evanescent states, \textit{independent} of the physical potential. Furthermore, the existence of non-relativistic and relativistic Momentunian \textit{partners} is demonstrated, whose zero-mode states are no longer necessarily zero energies, but vanishing momenta states. The natural emergence of the $1/2$-fractional time derivatives in the TSI QM, leads to supercharges which incorporate memory effects into the supersymmetric wave functions. Results indicate that supersymmetry emerges as a structural property of the TSI QM rather than being imposed phenomenologically.
summary: This paper shows that supersymmetry is an inherent feature of a quantum mechanical framework where space and time roles are inverted. By factorizing a specific 'Momentunian' operator, the authors derive a new class of SUSY that naturally incorporates memory effects through fractional calculus. This suggests that SUSY can be a structural consequence of space-time symmetry rather than an added assumption.
main_problem: Investigating whether supersymmetry (SUSY) can emerge as an intrinsic structural property of a space-time symmetric framework rather than being imposed phenomenologically.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that supersymmetry emerges naturally from the factorization of the 'Momentunian' operator within the Time-Space Inverted (TSI) quantum mechanics framework, leading to momentum doublets and memory effects via fractional derivatives.
method: The study employs Dirac's factorization procedure on the Momentunian operator, uses separation of variables for wavefunctions, and applies fractional calculus (Caputo sense) to handle 1/2-fractional time derivatives.
model_or_system: The framework is Time-Space Inverted (TSI) quantum mechanics, which treats time as an operator and position as a parameter, encompassing both non-relativistic and relativistic (Dirac-like) regimes.
key_observables: Momentum doublets (p and -p), zero-mode states (zero energy or zero momentum), and the Witten index.
important_parameters: Kinetic energy of time (Kt), rest mass (m0), speed of light (c), and the 1/2-fractional time derivative.
main_assumptions: The framework assumes a space-time symmetric (STS) structure where the roles of space and time are inverted.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the TSI QM framework, derives the Momentunian operator, demonstrates the factorization leading to emergent SUSY, explores both non-relativistic and relativistic cases, and provides a case study using the Harmonic Oscillator.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
